original_brief.headline: AB1751 Bans Cities from Charging Any Impact Fees
original_brief.subtext: Developers pay nothing. Existing taxpayers cover all new infrastructure costs.
original_brief.typographic_element: Bill number 'AB1751' as oversized display type, anchored upper-left, warm gold color
original_brief.optional_graphic: Faint outline of a California state map, bottom-right corner, as a subtle background element
original_brief.ai_image_prompt: Minimal flat design graphic on a deep navy blue background. A faint, semi-transparent outline silhouette of the state of California sits in the lower-right corner as a subtle compositional anchor, rendered in a slightly lighter shade of navy. A warm gold horizontal accent bar runs along the bottom edge of the composition. Clean, bold policy advocacy aesthetic with strong geometric structure. No text. No typography. No people. No logos.

briefs.reasoning: A Bill Spotlight needs to feel like a dossier dropped on a desk — authoritative, specific, confrontational. The bill number 'AB1751' must dominate as an oversized display element in warm gold, functioning less like a label and more like a case number stamped across evidence. The composition should be tight and left-anchored, creating density and urgency rather than the open breathing room that will define the Mission Frame post. The faint California map outline in the lower-right corner provides geographic grounding without competing with the typographic hierarchy — it tells the viewer this is happening here, in your state, right now. The warm gold accent stripe is subordinated to the bill number itself in this post, unlike a standard deployment, because the gold type IS the anchor — the stripe becomes a secondary structural element rather than the primary gold moment.
briefs.square_prompt:
Create a 1:1 square social media graphic (1080x1080px) for the California Stewardship Fund, a California policy advocacy organization. This is a Bill Spotlight card — it should feel like a breaking news dossier: tight, left-anchored, confrontational, authoritative.

BACKGROUND: Deep navy blue, hex #1a3a5c. Flat solid color. No gradients. No textures.

COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE: The layout is left-anchored and vertically stacked. All primary typographic elements align to a left margin set approximately 8-9% in from the left edge. The composition fills the frame with purpose — this is a dense, data-driven card, not an airy one.

TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY (top to bottom, reading order):

1. DOMINANT ELEMENT — Bill number: 'AB1751' rendered in an ultra-bold, condensed sans-serif display typeface. This text is enormous — it should occupy roughly 30-35% of the total frame height. Color: warm gold, hex #c9a227. Positioned in the upper portion of the frame, left-aligned, starting close to the top edge with minimal top padding (approximately 8% from top). The sheer scale of this text is confrontational — it is not a label, it is a declaration. The characters should feel like they are stamped or branded onto the card.

2. SECONDARY ELEMENT — Headline: 'AB1751 Bans Cities from Charging Any Impact Fees' rendered in a bold sans-serif at roughly 40-45% the size of the bill number display type. Color: white, hex #ffffff. Left-aligned, positioned directly below the bill number with tight leading — close enough that the two feel connected as a unit. This text may wrap to two lines if needed for legibility.

3. ACCENT STRIPE — A warm gold horizontal rule or bar, hex #c9a227, approximately 3-4px tall, spanning from the left margin to roughly 60-70% of the frame width. Positioned between the headline and the subtext, serving as a visual separator and structural anchor.

4. TERTIARY ELEMENT — Subtext: 'Developers pay nothing. Existing taxpayers cover all new infrastructure costs.' Rendered in a medium-weight sans-serif at approximately 55-60% the size of the headline text. Color: white, hex #ffffff, but at approximately 85% opacity to create a subtle hierarchy step down from the headline. Left-aligned, positioned below the accent stripe with comfortable breathing room.

OPTIONAL GRAPHIC ELEMENT — California state map outline: A very faint, barely-visible outline of the state of California, rendered as a simple geometric silhouette with no fill and a very thin stroke line. Color: white at approximately 8-10% opacity — it should read as a ghost or watermark presence, not a visible illustration. Position it in the lower-right quadrant of the frame, large enough that the shape is recognizable but small enough that it does not compete with any text. The bottom of the map shape should align near the bottom edge; the rightmost point should align near the right edge.

STYLE CONSTRAINTS: No photographs. No human figures. No logos. No icons. No decorative flourishes beyond what is specified. No gradients. The entire visual weight of this graphic comes from typographic scale and precision. The feeling is: a document of record, not a social media post trying to look like one.
briefs.landscape_prompt:
Create a 16:9 landscape social media graphic (1920x1080px) for the California Stewardship Fund, a California policy advocacy organization. This is a Bill Spotlight card — it should feel like a breaking news dossier: tight, left-anchored, confrontational, authoritative. The horizontal format is used to create a strong left-zone / right-zone split, with all typography commanding the left two-thirds of the frame and the California map element given deliberate negative space in the right third.

BACKGROUND: Deep navy blue, hex #1a3a5c. Flat solid color. No gradients. No textures.

COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE: The frame is divided into two conceptual zones. LEFT ZONE (approximately left 65% of the frame): All primary typography, left-aligned to a margin set approximately 6% in from the left edge. This zone is dense, purposeful, and authoritative. RIGHT ZONE (approximately right 35% of the frame): Occupied primarily by the faint California map outline and open navy negative space — this breathing room creates compositional contrast and allows the left zone's density to feel intentional rather than cluttered.

TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY (within the left zone, reading top to bottom):

1. DOMINANT ELEMENT — Bill number: 'AB1751' rendered in an ultra-bold, condensed sans-serif display typeface. In landscape format, this text is massive — approximately 28-32% of the total frame height, which given the wider frame creates a powerful horizontal anchor. Color: warm gold, hex #c9a227. Positioned in the upper-left area of the frame, with the top of the letterforms sitting approximately 10-12% down from the top edge. The scale is confrontational — this text reads from across a room. It is stamped, branded, declarative.

2. SECONDARY ELEMENT — Headline: 'AB1751 Bans Cities from Charging Any Impact Fees' rendered in bold sans-serif at approximately 38-42% the size of the bill number display type. Color: white, hex #ffffff. Left-aligned, positioned directly below the bill number with tight but readable leading. In landscape, this text will likely fit on one or two lines within the left zone without wrapping awkwardly — use the horizontal space to keep it tight and punchy.

3. ACCENT STRIPE — A warm gold horizontal rule or bar, hex #c9a227, approximately 3px tall, spanning from the left margin across the full left zone (approximately 65% of total frame width). Positioned between the headline and the subtext as a structural separator.

4. TERTIARY ELEMENT — Subtext: 'Developers pay nothing. Existing taxpayers cover all new infrastructure costs.' Rendered in medium-weight sans-serif at approximately 52-58% the size of the headline. Color: white, hex #ffffff, at approximately 85% opacity. Left-aligned, below the accent stripe. In landscape format, this text has room to breathe — do not stretch it across the full left zone; keep it contained to roughly the left 55% of the frame so it stays tight and readable.

OPTIONAL GRAPHIC ELEMENT — California state map outline: A very faint outline of the state of California as a simple geometric silhouette, no fill, thin stroke only. Color: white at approximately 9-11% opacity — a ghost presence, barely visible. In the landscape format, this element is centered within the right zone (roughly the rightmost 35% of the frame). Scale it so the map fills the right zone vertically from approximately 15% to 85% of the frame height, maintaining correct proportions for the California silhouette. The map should feel like a watermark — present but subordinate, grounding the card geographically without competing with a single word of type.

STYLE CONSTRAINTS: No photographs. No human figures. No logos. No icons. No decorative flourishes beyond what is specified. No gradients. The entire visual weight of this graphic comes from typographic scale, left-anchored density, and the disciplined contrast between the active left zone and the spare right zone. The feeling is: a document of record issued with authority.

original_brief.headline: AB2288 Forces Housing into Industrial Zones
original_brief.subtext: Cities lose authority over employment land and cannot require developer infrastructure funding.
original_brief.typographic_element: Bill number 'AB2288' as oversized display type, anchored upper-left, warm gold color
original_brief.optional_graphic: Simplified flat icon of an industrial building beside a residential building, separated by a vertical warm gold dividing line, centered lower half of composition
original_brief.ai_image_prompt: Minimal flat design graphic on a deep navy blue background. Two simple geometric building silhouettes side by side in the lower-center of the composition — one with a wide low industrial roofline on the left, one with a pitched residential roofline on the right — both rendered as clean white outlines. A warm gold vertical line separates them. A warm gold horizontal accent bar anchors the bottom edge. Bold, clean policy advocacy aesthetic. No text. No typography. No people. No logos.

briefs.reasoning: An Action Alert demands kinetic urgency — the composition must feel like it's moving toward the viewer, not waiting to be read. Where the Bill Spotlight (Post 1) uses authority and centered gravity to inform, this post uses asymmetric tension and a dominant upper-left anchor to mobilize. The warm gold is deployed as a bold diagonal slash element and as the oversized 'AB2288' display type — not as a passive stripe — creating forward momentum distinct from the Bill Spotlight's authoritative horizontal stripe and the Mission Frame's contemplative open space. The industrial/residential icon pair with the gold dividing line occupies the lower half, grounding the urgency in a concrete visual conflict.
briefs.square_prompt: Create a 1:1 square social media graphic (1080x1080px) for the California Stewardship Fund, a policy advocacy organization. This is an Action Alert graphic — it must feel urgent, kinetic, and imperative, like a rally poster demanding immediate attention. Background: flat solid deep navy blue (#1a3a5c). No gradients. No photos. No people. No logos. COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE: The frame is organized around asymmetric tension. A bold warm gold (#c9a227) diagonal slash band cuts from the upper-left corner downward at roughly 12 degrees, serving as a structural urgency element — not a stripe, but a dynamic angular accent block approximately 18px thick positioned behind and below the bill number. TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY: 'AB2288' is the dominant display element — oversized, approximately 96pt or larger, bold weight, warm gold (#c9a227), anchored to the upper-left quadrant with the text edge approximately 48px from the left margin and 52px from the top. It should fill roughly 40% of the frame's width. It is dramatically larger than everything below it — not merely bigger, emphatically larger. Below 'AB2288', separated by approximately 24px of vertical space, render the headline in white (#ffffff), bold weight, approximately 28pt, flush left, maximum 2 lines, reading: 'AB2288 Forces Housing into Industrial Zones'. Below the headline, in white (#ffffff), regular weight, approximately 15pt, flush left, maximum 3 lines, render the subtext: 'Cities lose authority over employment land and cannot require developer infrastructure funding.' ACCENT ELEMENT: The warm gold diagonal slash band (#c9a227) sits behind the 'AB2288' type block, extending from the upper-left corner diagonally across the upper third, creating a sense of motion and imminence — like a warning marker cutting across the frame. OPTIONAL GRAPHIC ELEMENT: In the lower half of the composition, centered horizontally, render a simplified flat icon pair: a flat industrial building silhouette (rectangular, with a small smokestack detail, in white at approximately 50% opacity) on the left, and a flat residential building silhouette (simple house profile, in white at approximately 50% opacity) on the right. Between them, a vertical warm gold (#c9a227) dividing line approximately 3px wide and 80px tall separates the two icons. The icons should be approximately 80px tall each, sitting comfortably in the lower third without competing with the typography. Style: clean, minimal, flat, professional policy-advocacy design. No gradients, no textures, no photos, no people, no logos, no decorative flourishes beyond specified elements.
briefs.landscape_prompt: Create a 16:9 landscape social media graphic (1920x1080px) for the California Stewardship Fund, a policy advocacy organization. This is an Action Alert graphic — it must feel urgent, kinetic, and imperative, like a rally poster demanding immediate attention. Background: flat solid deep navy blue (#1a3a5c). No gradients. No photos. No people. No logos. COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE: The landscape format is divided into two horizontal zones. The LEFT ZONE (approximately 58% of frame width) is the typographic command zone — dense, urgent, left-anchored. The RIGHT ZONE (approximately 42% of frame width) contains the icon graphic element and breathing room, but is punctuated by a bold warm gold diagonal slash that drives the eye leftward back into the type. A warm gold (#c9a227) diagonal slash band — approximately 20px thick — cuts across the full frame at roughly 10 degrees, positioned in the upper third of the composition, passing behind the 'AB2288' display type, creating a sense of kinetic motion across the horizontal expanse. TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY — LEFT ZONE: 'AB2288' is the dominant display element — oversized, approximately 110pt or larger, bold weight, warm gold (#c9a227), anchored to the upper-left with approximately 60px margin from left and 56px from top. It is dramatically larger than everything below it — commanding, not merely prominent. Below 'AB2288', separated by approximately 20px vertical space, render the headline in white (#ffffff), bold weight, approximately 32pt, flush left, maximum 2 lines: 'AB2288 Forces Housing into Industrial Zones'. Below the headline, approximately 16px vertical space, render the subtext in white (#ffffff), regular weight, approximately 16pt, flush left, maximum 2 lines: 'Cities lose authority over employment land and cannot require developer infrastructure funding.' ACCENT ELEMENT: The warm gold diagonal slash band (#c9a227) begins at the left edge of the frame in the upper quarter, cuts diagonally downward left-to-right at approximately 10 degrees, and terminates at the right edge — a single angular structural element that communicates urgency and forward momentum across the full width of the landscape format. OPTIONAL GRAPHIC ELEMENT — RIGHT ZONE: Centered vertically in the right zone, approximately 60% from the left edge of the frame, render a simplified flat icon pair: a flat industrial building silhouette (rectangular with a small smokestack detail, white at approximately 45% opacity) on the left of the pair, and a flat residential building silhouette (simple house profile, white at approximately 45% opacity) on the right of the pair. A vertical warm gold (#c9a227) dividing line approximately 3px wide and 100px tall separates the two icons. The icons should each be approximately 95px tall. The right zone's open negative space amplifies the urgency of the crowded left zone — the contrast between typographic density and open space is intentional and compositionally meaningful. Style: clean, minimal, flat, professional policy-advocacy design. No gradients, no textures, no photos, no people, no logos, no decorative flourishes beyond specified elements.

original_brief.headline: Sacramento Is Taking Over Local Housing Decisions
original_brief.subtext: Three new bills strip city authority and shift infrastructure costs to existing taxpayers.
original_brief.typographic_element: Three warm gold vertical bars of equal height, arranged left to right across the lower third, suggesting three bills — a minimal, abstract typographic accent rather than literal bill numbers
original_brief.optional_graphic: California Capitol dome silhouette, faint, centered at bottom of composition
original_brief.ai_image_prompt: Minimal flat design graphic on a deep navy blue background. A faint, semi-transparent silhouette of a classic capitol dome building sits centered along the bottom edge of the composition, rendered in a slightly lighter shade of navy. Three slim warm gold vertical rectangles of equal height are arranged in a row across the lower third of the image, evenly spaced, as abstract geometric accents. A warm gold horizontal rule anchors the very bottom edge. Clean, bold policy advocacy aesthetic with strong geometric structure and institutional gravitas. No text. No typography. No people. No logos.

briefs.reasoning: The Mission Frame needs to feel expansive and philosophical — the manifesto close to the week, not another alarm. Where Post 1 used dramatic bill-number scale to create alarm and Post 2 used compressed urgency, this composition earns its emotional weight through openness: generous negative space in the upper field, a headline that sits with authority rather than shouts, and the three vertical gold bars functioning as a quiet structural motif rather than a data element. The Capitol dome silhouette, rendered faint and centered at the bottom, grounds the abstraction in a real institutional target — Sacramento — without making the image literal or documentary. Typography dominates the upper two-thirds; the lower third belongs to the gold bars and dome, creating a visual grammar of 'this is what we're up against' beneath 'this is the truth we're naming.'
briefs.square_prompt:
Create a 1:1 square social media graphic for California Stewardship Fund, a policy advocacy organization. This is a Mission Frame post — the closing, thematic post of a weekly set. The emotional register is resolve and philosophical weight, not alarm. The composition should feel spacious, deliberate, and manifesto-like.

BACKGROUND: Flat solid deep navy blue (#1a3a5c). No gradients, no textures, no photographic elements. The background fills the entire canvas.

COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE:
The frame is divided into three vertical zones of intent — upper, middle, lower — with generous breathing room separating each.

UPPER ZONE (top 15% of frame): Empty negative space. This silence is intentional — it communicates perspective and scale.

MIDDLE ZONE (roughly 20% to 65% from top): Typography commands this space. The headline 'Sacramento Is Taking Over Local Housing Decisions' is the dominant typographic element. It is rendered in white (#ffffff), bold weight, large — filling roughly 55–60% of the frame width, centered horizontally, set in a clean modern sans-serif with generous line spacing between the two lines of text ('Sacramento Is Taking Over' on the first line, 'Local Housing Decisions' on the second). The type is large enough to feel declarative and final — not a data point, a truth.

Below the headline, with substantial breathing room of approximately 6–8% of frame height: the subtext 'Three new bills strip city authority and shift infrastructure costs to existing taxpayers.' rendered in white (#ffffff), at roughly 30–35% the size of the headline, centered, regular weight, set in the same sans-serif. This line reads clearly as the supporting evidence beneath the declaration.

LOWER ZONE (bottom 30% of frame): Three warm gold (#c9a227) vertical bars of equal height and equal width, evenly spaced and centered as a group across the lower third. Each bar is approximately 4–5% of frame width wide and 18–22% of frame height tall. They are arranged symmetrically, left to right, with equal gaps between them and equal margins on their outer sides. These bars are a typographic/structural accent — minimal and abstract — suggesting three bills without naming them. They sit above the very bottom of the frame, leaving room below them.

At the bottom center of the composition, partially overlapping behind the three gold bars or sitting just below them: an extremely faint California Capitol dome silhouette, rendered in white at approximately 8–10% opacity — barely visible, atmospheric rather than literal. It is centered horizontally and anchors the lower edge of the composition.

No accent stripe across the lower third — the three vertical bars replace that structural role.

TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY:
1. Headline 'Sacramento Is Taking Over Local Housing Decisions' — largest, white, bold, dominant
2. Subtext 'Three new bills strip city authority and shift infrastructure costs to existing taxpayers.' — significantly smaller, white, regular weight
3. Three gold vertical bars — structural accent, not text
4. Capitol dome silhouette — atmospheric background element, lowest visual priority

STYLE CONSTRAINTS: No photographs. No people. No faces. No logos. No gradients. No illustrated elements beyond the minimal Capitol dome silhouette. Typography and geometric elements only. Clean, minimal, professional policy-advocacy aesthetic.
briefs.landscape_prompt:
Create a 16:9 landscape social media graphic for California Stewardship Fund, a policy advocacy organization. This is a Mission Frame post — the closing, thematic post of a weekly set. The emotional register is resolve and philosophical weight, not alarm. The composition should feel expansive, deliberate, and manifesto-like — using the horizontal expanse as an asset rather than a challenge to fill.

BACKGROUND: Flat solid deep navy blue (#1a3a5c). No gradients, no textures, no photographic elements. The background fills the entire canvas.

COMPOSITIONAL STRUCTURE:
The landscape format uses a left-weighted typographic anchor with open negative space on the right — a horizontal grammar that reads as 'statement meets silence.' This is fundamentally different from a centered-stack square: the asymmetric placement communicates that the truth is being spoken into open space.

LEFT ZONE (left 60% of frame): Typography anchors here, left-aligned with a left margin of approximately 6–7% of frame width.

Vertically within the left zone, the headline 'Sacramento Is Taking Over Local Housing Decisions' occupies the upper-center vertical position — roughly centered vertically in the frame, perhaps slightly above true center. It is rendered in white (#ffffff), bold weight, large — the dominant typographic element in the entire composition. Set in a clean modern sans-serif. 'Sacramento Is Taking Over' occupies the first line, 'Local Housing Decisions' the second, left-aligned. The type should feel monumental — commanding roughly 50–55% of the frame height across its two lines.

Below the headline, with 5–7% of frame height breathing room: the subtext 'Three new bills strip city authority and shift infrastructure costs to existing taxpayers.' rendered in white (#ffffff), at approximately 28–32% the size of the headline, left-aligned, regular weight, same sans-serif. This is the evidence beneath the declaration.

RIGHT ZONE (right 40% of frame): Intentional breathing room — open navy space. This negative space is compositionally active: it communicates perspective, resolve, and scale. The viewer's eye rests here after reading the declaration on the left.

LOWER BAND (bottom 22% of frame, spanning full width): Three warm gold (#c9a227) vertical bars of equal height and equal width, positioned in the lower third. They are centered as a group horizontally across the full frame width — not confined to the left zone — so they anchor the entire composition rather than just the text side. Each bar is approximately 2.5–3% of frame width wide and 14–16% of frame height tall. Evenly spaced with equal gaps. They are a minimal, abstract structural accent suggesting three bills.

At the bottom center of the full frame, partially behind the three gold bars or sitting just below them: an extremely faint California Capitol dome silhouette, rendered in white at approximately 8–10% opacity — barely visible, atmospheric. Centered horizontally across the full frame width, anchoring the lower edge.

No solid accent stripe — the three vertical bars and the negative space replace that structural role.

TYPOGRAPHIC HIERARCHY:
1. Headline 'Sacramento Is Taking Over Local Housing Decisions' — largest, white, bold, left-anchored, dominant
2. Subtext 'Three new bills strip city authority and shift infrastructure costs to existing taxpayers.' — significantly smaller, white, regular weight, beneath headline
3. Three gold vertical bars — structural accent spanning full width, lower third
4. Capitol dome silhouette — atmospheric background element, lowest visual priority

STYLE CONSTRAINTS: No photographs. No people. No faces. No logos. No gradients. No illustrated elements beyond the minimal Capitol dome silhouette. Typography and geometric elements only. Clean, minimal, professional policy-advocacy aesthetic.
